summary: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default ChatGPT model, pairing a public product launch with a system card and positioning it around lower hallucination rates, clearer answers, and stronger personalization. Amazon added OS Level Actions to Bedrock AgentCore Browser, extending agents from web-page interaction to direct on-screen OS control via the InvokeBrowser API. Google also upgraded Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1, enabling more complex multi-step smart-home commands and signaling continued competition to own the consumer AI assistant layer.

top_topics.title: Introducing OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser
top_topics.summary: AWS introduced OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser. The new capability gives agents direct operating-system control through the InvokeBrowser API so they can interact with content beyond standard browser surfaces.

items.title: Intelligence-driven message defense and insights using Amazon Bedrock
items.summary: In this post, you will learn how you can use Amazon Nova Foundation Models in Amazon Bedrock to apply generative AI techniques for both business protection and enhancement. You can identify obvious and disguised attempts at direct contact while gaining valuable insights into customer sentiment and service improvement opportunities.

items.title: Secure AI agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity on Amazon ECS
items.summary: AI agents in production require secure access to external services. Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity, available as a standalone service, secures how your AI agents access external services whether they run on compute platforms like Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, AWS Lambda, or on-premises. This post implements Authorization Code Grant (3-legged OAuth) on Amazon ECS with secure session binding and scoped tokens.

items.title: Introducing OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser
items.summary: We’re announcing OS Level Actions for AgentCore Browser. This new capability unblocks these scenarios by exposing direct OS control through the InvokeBrowser API, so agents can interact with content visible on the screen, not only what's accessible through the browser's web layer. By combining full-desktop screenshots with mouse and keyboard control at the OS level, agents can observe native UI, reason about it, and act on it within the same session. This post walks through how OS Level Actions work, what actions are supported, and how to get started.

items.title: How Hapag-Lloyd uses Amazon Bedrock to transform customer feedback into actionable insights
items.summary: Hapag-Lloyd's Digital Customer Experience and Engineering team, distributed between Hamburg and Gdańsk, drives digital innovation by developing and maintaining customer-facing web and mobile products. In this post, we walk you through our generative AI–powered feedback analysis solution built using Amazon Bedrock, Elasticsearch, and open-source frameworks like LangChain and LangGraph

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: AWS introduced OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser. The new capability gives agents direct operating-system control through the InvokeBrowser API so they can interact with content beyond standard browser surfaces.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Amazon is expanding Bedrock agents from browser-only automation to fuller desktop-style control, which materially broadens the range of enterprise workflows agents can execute.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser Adds OS Level Actions via InvokeBrowser API
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: AWS said the new OS Level Actions capability exposes direct operating-system control through the InvokeBrowser API, allowing agents to interact with content visible on screen rather than only the browser DOM.
The launch targets production agent use cases where critical UI elements are inaccessible through standard web-layer automation, a common blocker for enterprise workflow execution.
The same Bedrock AgentCore push also highlighted AgentCore Identity as a standalone service for securing agent access across Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, AWS Lambda, and on-premises environments.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: OpenAI announced GPT-5.5 Instant as an update to ChatGPT's default model. The company says it improves answer quality, reduces hallucinations, and adds stronger personalization controls.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This is a direct platform-level model upgrade from OpenAI that can immediately shift user expectations, enterprise evaluation baselines, and competitive product roadmaps.
top_topics.HEADLINE: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s New Default Model
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: OpenAI positioned GPT-5.5 Instant around smarter and more accurate responses, with explicit claims of reduced hallucinations versus the prior default ChatGPT experience.
The release includes both the product announcement and a GPT-5.5 Instant System Card, giving buyers and researchers an official safety and deployment artifact to review alongside the launch.
OpenAI said GPT-5.5 Instant improves personalization controls, a commercially important feature for retention and for tailoring assistant behavior across consumer and enterprise use cases.

items.summary: Google Home users can now ask Gemini to complete more complex, multi-step tasks and combine multiple tasks in a single command. items.title: Google Home gets upgraded Gemini voice assistant and new camera controls
items.summary: Google's smart home ecosystem is getting its biggest update since the AI-fueled 2025 revamp.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Google updated Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1 and expanded Google Home's ability to handle more complex, multi-step requests. Users can now combine multiple tasks into a single command.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Google is strengthening Gemini’s role as an ambient consumer assistant, a strategic battleground for device ecosystems, subscription upsell, and home automation lock-in.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Google Upgrades Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1 for Multi-Step Smart-Home Commands
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Google Home users can now issue more complex requests that combine multiple actions in a single command, moving the assistant closer to agent-like orchestration in the home.
The upgrade to Gemini 3.1 is intended to improve how Google Home interprets and executes requests, addressing a core reliability issue in voice-driven smart-home control.
Coverage described the release as Google’s biggest smart-home update since its 2025 AI revamp, indicating this is a meaningful platform iteration rather than a minor feature patch.

topics.title: Our AI started a cafe in Stockholm
topics.summary:
Our AI started a cafe in Stockholm

Andon Labs previously started an AI-run retail store in San Francisco. Now they're running a similar experiment in Stockholm, Sweden, only this time it's a cafe.

These experiments are interesting, and often throw out amusing anecdotes:

During the first week of inventory, Mona ordered 120 eggs even though the café has no stove. When the staff told her they couldn’t cook them, she suggested using the high-speed oven, until they pointed out the eggs would likely explode. She also tried to solve the problem of fresh tomatoes being spoiled too fast by ordering 22.5 kg of canned tomatoes fo...

topics.title: Peter Sarlin’s QuTwo reaches $380M valuation in angel round
topics.summary: QyTw0, the Finnish AI lab founded by former AMD Silo AI CEO Peter Sarlin, is now valued at €325 million (approximately $380 million) after raising a €25 million angel round ($29 million). It's a sign of enduring tailwinds for AI, quantum computing, and sovereign tech, especially for Europe-made companies.

topics.title: Granite 4.1 3B SVG Pelican Gallery
topics.summary:
Granite 4.1 3B SVG Pelican Gallery

IBM released their Granite 4.1 family of LLMs a few days ago. They're Apache 2.0 licensed and come in 3B, 8B and 30B sizes.

Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built by Granite team member Yousaf Shah describes the training process in detail.

Unsloth released the unsloth/granite-4.1-3b-GGUF collection of GGUF encoded quantized variants of the 3B model - 21 different model files ranging in size from 1.2GB to 6.34GB.

All 21 of those Unsloth fil...

topics.title: Component-Aware Self-Speculative Decoding in Hybrid Language Models
topics.summary: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive inference by drafting candidate tokens with a fast model and verifying them in parallel with the target. Self-speculative methods avoid the need for an external drafter but have been studied exclusively in homogeneous Transformer architectures. We introduce component-aware self-speculative decoding, the first method to exploit the internal architectural heterogeneity of hybrid language models, isolating the SSM/linear-attention subgraph as a zero-cost internal draft. We evaluate this on two architecturally distinct hybrid families: Falcon-H1 (...

topics.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
topics.summary: Adapting large pretrained models to diverse tasks is now routine, yet the two dominant strategies of parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) and low-rank compression are typically composed in sequence. This decoupled practice first compresses and then fine-tunes adapters, potentially misaligning the compressed subspace with downstream objectives and squandering a global parameter budget. To overcome this limitation, we introduce JACTUS (Joint Adaptation and Compression with a Task-aware Union of Subspaces), a single framework that unifies compression and adaptation. From a small calibration...

items.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
items.summary: Adapting large pretrained models to diverse tasks is now routine, yet the two dominant strategies of parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) and low-rank compression are typically composed in sequence. This decoupled practice first compresses and then fine-tunes adapters, potentially misaligning the compressed subspace with downstream objectives and squandering a global parameter budget. To overcome this limitation, we introduce JACTUS (Joint Adaptation and Compression with a Task-aware Union of Subspaces), a single framework that unifies compression and adaptation. From a small calibration...

metadata.comment: Jointly chooses compression and adaptation subspaces instead of compress-then-finetune, with global rank allocation under a parameter budget.

paper_spotlight.label: New Frontier Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that appear to open a genuinely new direction, paradigm, or field.

items.title: Understanding Self-Supervised Learning via Latent Distribution Matching
items.summary: The paper proposes a broad unifying theoretical lens for self-supervised learning that could shape future representation-learning work beyond a single method.
items.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes a broad unifying theoretical lens for self-supervised learning that could shape future representation-learning work beyond a single method.

items.title: Learning to Theorize the World from Observation
items.summary: It proposes a notably new direction for world models: explicit theory induction from observation.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: It proposes a notably new direction for world models: explicit theory induction from observation.

items.title: Intervention Complexity as a Canonical Reward and a Measure of Intelligence
items.summary: The paper proposes a new formal reward primitive and intelligence characterization, which is a paradigm-level conceptual contribution.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes a new formal reward primitive and intelligence characterization, which is a paradigm-level conceptual contribution.

items.title: Analytic Bridge Diffusions for Controlled Path Generation
items.summary: The paper proposes a rare closed-form alternative to neural bridge diffusion and could seed a new analytic reference line for transport and controlled generative dynamics.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes a rare closed-form alternative to neural bridge diffusion and could seed a new analytic reference line for transport and controlled generative dynamics.

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: SplitZip: Ultra Fast Lossless KV Compression for Disaggregated LLM Serving
items.summary: Broadly important today: Introduces a GPU-friendly lossless KV-cache codec tailored to prefill-decode disaggregation by exploiting exponent redundancy in KV activations.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Introduces a GPU-friendly lossless KV-cache codec tailored to prefill-decode disaggregation by exploiting exponent redundancy in KV activations.

items.title: On the Invariants of Softmax Attention
items.summary: Broadly important today: Identifies algebraic and empirical invariants of softmax attention via the row-centered energy field, including rank bounds and key incoherence.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Identifies algebraic and empirical invariants of softmax attention via the row-centered energy field, including rank bounds and key incoherence.

items.title: eOptShrinkQ: Near-Lossless KV Cache Compression Through Optimal Spectral Denoising and Quantization
items.summary: Broadly important today: Combines spectral denoising with quantization for near-lossless KV-cache compression using a low-rank shared-context plus isotropic residual decomposition.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Combines spectral denoising with quantization for near-lossless KV-cache compression using a low-rank shared-context plus isotropic residual decomposition.

items.title: Boundary Mass and the Soft-to-Hard Limit in Mixture-of-Experts
items.summary: Broadly important today: Provides geometric boundary-mass theory for the singular soft-to-hard temperature limit in softmax-routed MoE.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Provides geometric boundary-mass theory for the singular soft-to-hard temperature limit in softmax-routed MoE.

items.title: ZeRO-Prefill: Zero Redundancy Overheads in MoE Prefill Serving
items.summary: MoE serving efficiency is a broadly important systems topic, and the paper proposes a concrete new execution regime rather than routine optimization.
items.spotlight_comment: MoE serving efficiency is a broadly important systems topic, and the paper proposes a concrete new execution regime rather than routine optimization.

items.title: Transformers with Selective Access to Early Representations
items.summary: Broadly important today: Context-dependent gating gives later transformer layers selective access to first-layer values, treating early-feature reuse as retrieval.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Context-dependent gating gives later transformer layers selective access to first-layer values, treating early-feature reuse as retrieval.

x_buzz.summary: Google Home users can now ask Gemini to complete more complex, multi-step tasks and combine multiple tasks in a single command.
